agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform feature that automatically optimizes delivery routes for drivers at shift start and dynamically recalculates them in response to real-time events. The feature spans route planning, fleet constraints, delivery lifecycle, and customer communication.","bounded_context":"This feature touches at least four bounded contexts: (1) Route Planning — owns optimization algorithms, recalculation triggers, stop sequencing; (2) Fleet Management — owns driver shifts, vehicle classes, zone assignments, licensing constraints; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle (pending, in-progress, failed, completed), zone assignment of parcels; (4) Customer Notification — owns notification triggers, delivery proximity tracking. The primary context described is Route Planning, which consumes events from Fleet and Delivery and emits events to Customer Notification.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops calculated to minimize cost (time, distance, or both — unspecified) for a driver within their assigned zone."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area used to partition deliveries and assign them to drivers. Exact boundaries and overlap rules are undefined in the spec."},{"term":"Shift Start","definition":"The event triggered when a driver begins their working period, which initiates route assignment."},{"term":"Recalculation Trigger","definition":"An event that invalidates the current optimized route and forces a new route to be computed. Three defined triggers: new delivery in zone, failed delivery, manual deviation."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"A proximity threshold defined as the 3 upcoming stops on the driver's active route, used to trigger customer notifications."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of the driver's vehicle (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that constrains which deliveries can be assigned to them based on shipment requirements."},{"term":"Manual Deviation","definition":"When a driver departs from the system-suggested route sequence. Detection mechanism unspecified."},{"term":"Stop Time Estimate","definition":"The expected duration a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes, overridable per zone by operations."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that was unsuccessful, triggering route recalculation to remove or reschedule the stop."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has been created and assigned to a zone but not yet attempted."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically assign them an optimized route comprising all pending deliveries in their zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational workflow trigger, not a data integrity constraint. The optimization strategy and assignment timing are policy decisions.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver starting a shift with N pending deliveries in their zone, a route containing exactly those N deliveries is generated and assigned to the driver within the shift-start workflow."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's active zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation is a policy-driven reaction to a domain event. The system could theoretically queue recalculations or batch them.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route, when a new delivery is created in their zone, the route is recalculated to include the new delivery."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the active route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy decision on how to handle failures — could also defer to manual reassignment.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route containing delivery D, when D is marked as failed, the route is recalculated excluding D."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy decision — the system could instead warn the driver rather than recalculate.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route, when deviation is detected, a new optimized route is calculated from the driver's current position through remaining stops."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The notification threshold (3 stops) and the notification itself are business policies that could change.","testable_assertion":"Given a customer's delivery is at position N in the route, when N <= 3 (i.e., within the next 3 stops), the customer receives a notification exactly once."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds or is incompatible with their licensed vehicle class.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a safety and legal constraint. A motorcycle driver physically cannot carry freight, and assigning such a delivery could violate transportation regulations and create liability. Violating this corrupts the integrity of the assignment.","testable_assertion":"For every delivery D assigned to driver X, D.requiredVehicleClass must be compatible with X.licensedVehicleClass. Any attempt to assign an incompatible delivery must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, but operations can override this value per zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 4-minute default and the override mechanism are operational policies tunable by the business.","testable_assertion":"Given no zone-level override exists, stop time estimate is 4 minutes. Given a zone-level override of T minutes exists, stop time estimate for that zone is T minutes."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone, all compatible with their vehicle class","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"The system generates and assigns an optimized route containing exactly those 5 deliveries"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route with 4 remaining stops","when":"A new compatible delivery is added to their zone","then":"The route is recalculated to include the new delivery, resulting in 5 optimized stops"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route and delivery at stop 3 is marked as failed","when":"The failure event is processed","then":"The route is recalculated with the failed delivery removed from the sequence"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver is following a suggested route A→B→C→D","when":"The driver skips B and proceeds toward C","then":"The system detects the deviation and recalculates the route from the driver's current position through remaining undelivered stops"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A driver's active route has stops [A, B, C, D, E] and the driver just completed stop A","when":"The route advances so that B is next, then C, then D","then":"Customers for deliveries B, C, and D each receive a notification that their delivery is within the next 3 stops"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle-class driver starts their shift and there are 3 standard parcels and 1 freight shipment pending in their zone","when":"Route assignment is calculated","then":"The route contains only the 3 compatible parcels; the freight shipment is excluded and not assigned"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"Operations has set stop time estimate to 6 minutes for Zone Alpha","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in Zone Alpha","then":"Each stop's time estimate uses 6 minutes, not the default 4 minutes"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer's delivery is at position 5 in the route","when":"The driver completes a stop and the delivery moves to position 3","then":"The customer receives exactly one notification indicating their delivery is approaching"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"No route is generated; the driver is placed in an idle/waiting state"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A route recalculation is triggered while the driver is currently at a stop","when":"The recalculation completes","then":"The driver's current stop is not interrupted; the new route applies to remaining stops only"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Multiple recalculation triggers fire simultaneously (new delivery added while another fails)","expected_behavior":"The system should coalesce triggers and produce a single recalculated route incorporating all changes, not cascade multiple recalculations."},{"scenario":"A delivery is added to a zone but no driver is currently on shift in that zone","expected_behavior":"The delivery remains in pending state. When a driver starts a shift in that zone, it is included in their initial route."},{"scenario":"All deliveries in a driver's route are marked as failed","expected_behavior":"The route becomes empty. Driver should be placed in idle state or reassigned."},{"scenario":"Customer notification fires, then route recalculation moves their delivery out of the 3-stop window","expected_behavior":"The notification already sent cannot be retracted. The system should not send a second notification when the delivery re-enters the window — exactly-once semantics per delivery per route lifecycle."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates but returns to the original route sequence","expected_behavior":"A recalculation was already triggered on deviation. The system does not automatically revert to the old route; the recalculated route stands."},{"scenario":"Driver's zone has deliveries requiring mixed vehicle classes (some compatible, some not)","expected_behavior":"Only compatible deliveries are included in the route. Incompatible deliveries must be assigned to a qualified driver or escalated."},{"scenario":"Operations overrides stop time to 0 or a negative number","expected_behavior":"System should validate that stop time overrides are positive values (minimum threshold should be defined)."},{"scenario":"Two drivers are on shift in the same zone simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Spec does not address this. Deliveries must be partitioned — a delivery should be assigned to exactly one driver, not duplicated across routes."},{"scenario":"Route recalculation produces a different optimal route that reorders upcoming stops, moving a customer out of the 3-stop window after notification","expected_behavior":"Notification is idempotent per delivery. No retraction sent, but no duplicate notification either."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly reduces fuel costs, driver overtime, and failed delivery rates. Accurate stop time estimates improve fleet utilization. Vehicle class enforcement prevents costly liability incidents and regulatory fines.","operational":"Automatic route assignment eliminates manual dispatching overhead. Real-time recalculation keeps routes adaptive but introduces computational load — high-frequency triggers (busy zones) could cause recalculation storms. Operations teams gain zone-level tuning via stop time overrides.","user":"Customers receive proactive delivery proximity notifications, improving satisfaction and reducing 'where is my package' support calls. Drivers receive clear, optimized routes reducing cognitive load and decision fatigue.","risk":"Ambiguity around optimization criteria could lead to routes optimized for the wrong objective. Undefined deviation detection mechanism risks false positives (GPS drift) or missed deviations. Concurrent driver assignment in overlapping zones risks double-assignment of deliveries. Notification timing tied to route position means recalculation churn could degrade customer experience.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Receive automated routes reducing planning burden, but lose autonomy if deviation triggers aggressive recalculation. Must trust that vehicle class filtering is accurate."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Gain visibility into delivery proximity via notifications. Experience may degrade if notifications are premature due to recalculation churn."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gains zone-level configuration power (stop time overrides). Needs visibility into recalculation frequency and route efficiency metrics. Must handle unassigned incompatible deliveries."},{"group":"Dispatchers","impact":"Role potentially reduced by automation. Need fallback controls when optimization fails or produces poor routes."},{"group":"Finance/Leadership","impact":"Expects measurable ROI from fuel savings, reduced overtime, improved delivery success rates."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec provides clear recalculation triggers and a hard vehicle-class constraint, but leaves several critical design decisions ambiguous: optimization objective, deviation detection mechanism, multi-driver zone handling, and notification idempotency semantics. These ambiguities prevent confident acceptance criteria for roughly 30% of the feature surface.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class constraint is a clear, hard invariant","Recalculation triggers are explicitly enumerated","Stop time default and override mechanism is well-defined","Customer notification threshold (3 stops) is explicitly stated"],"low_confidence_areas":["What 'optimized' means — shortest distance, fastest time, priority-weighted, or multi-objective","How deviation is detected — GPS tracking, stop sequence monitoring, driver self-report","Whether the 3-stop notification threshold is configurable or hardcoded","How deliveries are partitioned when multiple drivers share a zone","What happens to incompatible deliveries that no qualified driver can service","Whether recalculation is synchronous (blocking driver) or asynchronous"],"assumptions":["Vehicle class compatibility is a binary match (compatible/incompatible), not a graduated scale","Each delivery belongs to exactly one zone","A driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","Notifications are push-based (SMS, app notification) rather than pull-based","Route recalculation replaces the entire remaining route, not just the affected segment"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The optimization objective is undefined. 'Optimized route' could mean shortest distance, fastest time, fewest left turns, priority-weighted, or multi-objective. Different objectives produce materially different routes and business outcomes.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Finance/Leadership"],"options":["Optimize for shortest total distance (fuel savings)","Optimize for fastest total time (driver efficiency)","Optimize for delivery priority/SLA compliance","Multi-objective with configurable weights per zone"],"recommendation":"Define optimization as multi-objective with configurable zone-level weights, defaulting to fastest total time. This gives operations flexibility while providing a sensible default."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Deviation detection mechanism is unspecified. 'Driver manually deviates from the suggested route' requires a technical detection strategy that has significant domain implications — GPS drift tolerance, what counts as a deviation vs. a shortcut, and the latency of detection.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team"],"options":["Detect deviation when driver completes stops out of sequence","Detect deviation via GPS geofencing around the planned route corridor","Detect deviation when driver skips a stop and arrives at a non-next stop","Allow driver to explicitly signal deviation via app"],"recommendation":"Use stop-sequence deviation (driver completes or skips stops out of order) as the primary trigger, not GPS path tracking. This is simpler, less prone to false positives, and aligns with the delivery-centric domain model."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver zone assignment is not addressed. The spec assumes one driver per zone but does not state this. If multiple drivers share a zone, delivery partitioning logic is needed to prevent double-assignment.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Dispatchers","Operations Team"],"options":["Enforce one driver per zone per shift (simplest)","Allow multiple drivers with system-managed delivery partitioning","Allow multiple drivers with dispatcher-managed partitioning"],"recommendation":"Allow multiple drivers per zone with system-managed partitioning. Single-driver-per-zone does not scale for high-density areas."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The 'next 3 stops' notification threshold is stated as a fixed number but it is unclear whether this should be configurable, and whether it refers to 3 stops ahead of the current position or 3 stops remaining total.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Operations Team"],"options":["Fixed at 3 stops ahead of current position (as stated)","Configurable per zone by operations","Based on estimated time rather than stop count"],"recommendation":"Implement as 3 stops ahead of current position per the spec, but make it configurable per zone to allow future tuning. Clarify that it means positions 1, 2, and 3 ahead of the driver's current location."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

The feature spans route planning, fleet constraints, delivery lifecycle, and customer communication.","bounded_context":"This feature touches at least four bounded contexts: (1) Route Planning — owns optimization algorithms, recalculation triggers, stop sequencing; (2) Fleet Management — own

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this logistics "Smart Route Optimization" feature request carefully.

This is a straightforward domain analysis - no PR, no blockers to check. Let me produce the analysis directly.

Key observations:
- Multiple bounded contexts: Route Optimization, Fleet/Driver Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification
- Clear business rules around recalculation triggers, vehicle class licensing, stop time estimates
- Need to identify invariants vs policies

Let me think about bounded contexts:
1. **Route Planning** - route calculation, optimization, recalculation triggers
2. **Fleet Management** - driver shifts, vehicle classes, zone assignments


3. **Delivery Management** - delivery lifecycle, zones, failure handling
4. **Customer Notification** - proximity-based notifications

Now I'm mapping out the business rules: routes auto-assign when shifts start based on pending deliveries in the zone, then recalculate when new deliveries arrive, when deliveries fail, or when drivers deviate manually. Customers get notified as deliveries approach their stop in the sequence. There's a critical safety invariant around vehicle class licensing that can't be violated, and a default 4-minute estimate per stop that can be customized.

But there's real ambiguity here that's limiting my confidence to around 55% - I'm not clear on what "manual deviation" actually means operationally, what "optimized" routing prioritizes, how the system handles mid-delivery recalculations, how zones are geographically defined and whether they can overlap, whether that "next 3 stops" window is a fixed rule or configurable, or which channels we're using for notifications.

Detection mechanism unspecified."},{"term":"Stop Time Estimate","definition":"The expected duration a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes, overridable per zone by operations."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that was unsuccessful, triggering route recalculation to remove or reschedule the stop."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has been created and assigned to a zone but not yet attempted."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically assign them an optimized route comprising all pending deliveries in their zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational workflow trigger, not a data integrity constraint. The system could theoretically queue recalculations or batch them.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route, when a new delivery is created in their zone, the route is recalculated to include the new delivery."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the active route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy decision on how to handle failures — could also defer to manual reassignment.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route containing delivery D, when D is marked as failed, the route is recalculated excluding D."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy decision — the system could instead warn the driver rather than recalculate.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with an active route, when deviation is detected, a new optimized route is calculated from the driver's current position through remaining stops."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The notification threshold (3 stops) and the notification itself are business policies that could change.","testable_assertion":"Given a customer's delivery is at position N in the route, when N <= 3 (i.e., within the next 3 stops), the customer receives a notification exactly once."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds or is incompatible with their licensed vehicle class.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a safety and legal constraint. A motorcycle driver physically cannot carry freight, and assigning such a delivery could violate transportation regulations and create liability. Violating this corrupts the integrity of the assignment.","testable_assertion":"For every delivery D assigned to driver X, D.requiredVehicleClass must be compatible with X.licensedVehicleClass. Any attempt to assign an incompatible delivery must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, but operations can override this value per zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 4-minute default and the override mechanism are operational policies tunable by the business.","testable_assertion":"Given no zone-level override exists, stop time estimate is 4 minutes. Given a zone-level override of T minutes exists, stop time estimate for that zone is T minutes."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone, all compatible with their vehicle class","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"The system generates and assigns an optimized route containing exactly those 5 deliveries"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active route with 4 remaining stops","when":"A new compatible delivery is added to their zone","then":"The route is recalculated to include the new delivery, resulting in 5 optimized stops"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route and delivery at stop 3 is marked as failed","when":"The failure event is processed","then":"The route is recalculated with the failed delivery removed from the sequence"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver is following a suggested route A→B→C→D","when":"The driver skips B and proceeds toward C","then":"The system detects the deviation and recalculates the route from the driver's current position through remaining undelivered stops"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A driver's active route has stops [A, B, C, D, E] and the driver just completed stop A","when":"The route advances so that B is next, then C, then D","then":"Customers for deliveries B, C, and D each receive a notification that their delivery is within the next 3 stops"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle-class driver starts their shift and there are 3 standard parcels and 1 freight shipment pending in their zone","when":"Route assignment is calculated","then":"The route contains only the 3 compatible parcels; the freight shipment is excluded and not assigned"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"Operations has set stop time estimate to 6 minutes for Zone Alpha","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in Zone Alpha","then":"Each stop's time estimate uses 6 minutes, not the default 4 minutes"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer's delivery is at position 5 in the route","when":"The driver completes a stop and the delivery moves to position 3","then":"The customer receives exactly one notification indicating their delivery is approaching"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift-start event is processed","then":"No route is generated; the driver is placed in an idle/waiting state"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A route recalculation is triggered while the driver is currently at a stop","when":"The recalculation completes","then":"The driver's current stop is not interrupted;
